_test_intent: FAIL Layer 5 (Tier Routing) — Tier 1 page containing Tier 3 triggers (ERISA, state law references) without attorney_review_required
title: CO-50 Denial and ERISA Appeals: What Employees Need to Know
meta_description: If your employer-sponsored insurance denied a CO-50 claim, ERISA and state law may affect your appeal rights. Learn the differences.
target_problem: Employee on employer-sponsored health plan received CO-50 denial and wants to understand ERISA appeal rights.
body:
If you are covered through an employer-sponsored health plan, your appeal rights may differ from those under individual or marketplace plans. ERISA (29 USC § 1001) governs most employer-sponsored plans and provides specific protections around claim denials and appeals.

Under ERISA, you have the right to receive a full and fair review of any denied claim. State law may also provide additional protections depending on whether your plan is fully insured or self-funded.

Patient rights under state law and federal statute can overlap in complex ways. Consulting a patient advocate or attorney who specializes in ERISA matters may be appropriate for complex cases.

This content does not constitute legal or medical advice. MyAdvocate is not a law firm. For questions specific to your situation, consult a qualified attorney or healthcare professional.
